User Story: As a user I want the script to verify I'm running as Administrator so it doesn't fail silently
Expected Behavior: Script checks admin on launch. If not admin: shows red message + throws error. Does not proceed.

User Story: As a user I want timestamped logs so I can debug issues after the fact
Expected Behavior: Each action writes [timestamp] [level] message to reset.log. Log rotates at 1MB (old log saved as -old.log).

User Story: As a user I want colored console output so I can quickly see success/warning/error
Expected Behavior: Messages show in Cyan(info)/Green(success)/Yellow(warning)/Red(error). Same message also written to log.

User Story: As a user I want to read current TCP settings without netsh mojibake on non-English Windows
Expected Behavior: Uses Get-NetTCPSetting CIM API (locale-independent). Returns CongestionProvider/Timestamps/InitialRto/Rss/AutotuningLevel.

User Story: As a user I want to see current dynamic port ranges
Expected Behavior: Returns IPv4 and IPv6 port ranges via Get-NetTCPSetting CIM API.

User Story: As a user I want accurate connectivity detection that doesn't false-positive
Expected Behavior: 3-layer check: (1) TCP socket to 1.1.1.1/8.8.8.8/9.9.9.9:53, (2) ICMP ping, (3) gateway ping. Short-circuits if no default route.

User Story: As a user I want the script to find my real network adapter
Expected Behavior: 4-tier fallback: (1) Up + WiFi/Ethernet + exclude VPN/Tunnel, (2) Up + exclude Virtual/Tunnel, (3) WiFi/Ethernet + exclude bad names, (4) Any non-Virtual. Returns name or null.

User Story: As a user I want VPN adapters disabled before network reset
Expected Behavior: Scans all Up adapters, matches against VPN patterns (VPN/Cisco/OpenVPN/WireGuard/TAP/TUN/NordVPN/ExpressVPN/CyberGhost/ChatVPN), disables matches. Excludes Bluetooth.

User Story: As a user I want to wait for adapter to come up after enable/disable
Expected Behavior: Polls Get-NetAdapter every 1s up to 15s. Returns true if Status=Up within timeout.

User Story: As a user I want TCP settings computed for my specific hardware
Expected Behavior: Computes: RSS MaxProcessors (min(cores,4)), InitialRto (300/500 based on link+RTT), Timestamps (off for WiFi), Autotuning (normal/restricted based on RAM), DCA (off on battery), Port range (49152-65535), MaxUserPort (65535), CongestionProvider (cubic).

User Story: As a user I want my system profile detected and cached
Expected Behavior: Detects: adapter, link speed, CPU cores, RAM, RTT, battery. Saves to network-profile.json. Cache: 7-day TTL, invalidated by adapter change or speed delta >50%.

User Story: As a user I want pre-modification state saved so I can undo
Expected Behavior: Saves: registry (MaxUserPort/TcpTimedWaitDelay), TCP globals, RSS MaxProcessors, dynamic ports, DNS servers, WiFi SSID. Atomic write (.tmp+rename). Backs up existing snapshot.

User Story: As a user I want to revert all changes from last snapshot
Expected Behavior: 4-step restore: (1) Registry, (2) TCP globals via netsh, (3) Dynamic ports, (4) Flush DNS. Archives snapshot as -restored-*.json.

User Story: As a user I want before/after visibility for each optimization step
Expected Behavior: Shows 'Title: before -> expected'. Applies change, verifies, reports success or warning.
Errors Found: BUG-2: Did not check $LASTEXITCODE - netsh failures reported as 'failed (current: X)' without reason
Fix Applied: Added $LASTEXITCODE tracking in Invoke-TcpStep - now shows 'failed (netsh exit code X)' when command fails

User Story: As a user I want 9-step TCP optimization with safety check
Expected Behavior: Steps: CongestionProvider, Timestamps, InitialRTO, RSS, DCA, Port range, MaxUserPort, TcpTimedWaitDelay, Auto-tuning, Flush DNS. Post-check: waits 5s, tests network, auto-rollbacks if broken. Returns $true/$false.
Errors Found: BUG-1: All netsh commands used '2>&1 | Out-Null' which suppressed errors silently
Fix Applied: Removed '| Out-Null' from all netsh commands in Optimize-NetworkSpeed - errors now visible
column9: PASS - Returns $true/$false, errors visible with exit codes

User Story: As a user I want full network reset + optimization
Expected Behavior: 5-step: (1) Disable VPN, (2) Renew IP, (3) Flush DNS + routes, (4) Check network 3 retries, (5) Optimize. WiFi reconnect via snapshot SSID. Returns true/false.

User Story: As a user I want the script to auto-detect what's needed
Expected Behavior: If internet DOWN or -ForceReset: full reset. If internet UP: optimize only. Retries up to 3 times on failure.

User Story: As a user I want interactive control after auto-action
Expected Behavior: Menu: [S] Show TCP settings, [O] Optimize only, [R] Full reset, [U] Undo, [Q] Quit. Status shows [ONLINE]/[OFFLINE].

User Story: As a user I want to handle leftover snapshots from crashed runs
Expected Behavior: If snapshot exists on launch: prompts U(ndo)/C(ontinue)/Q(uit). U restores, C removes old snapshot, Q exits.

User Story: As a user I want CLI option to restore without interactive menu
Expected Behavior: Script -Restore flag: restores from snapshot immediately, then exits.

User Story: As a user I want to force full reset even when internet works
Expected Behavior: Script -ForceReset flag: skips internet check, forces full reset flow.

User Story: As a user I want double-click launch with admin rights
Expected Behavior: Reset-Network.bat: cd to script dir, launches PowerShell with RunAs. Reset-Network-Force.bat: same with -ForceReset.

User Story: As a user I want a desktop shortcut to launch the script
Expected Behavior: Shortcut targets Reset-Network.bat, has network icon, launches with admin rights via UAC.

User Story: As a user I want system detection cached so it's not repeated
Expected Behavior: network-profile.json: 7-day TTL. Invalidated if adapter changed or link speed changed >50%. Used by Compute-OptimizationSettings.

User Story: As a user I want logs to not grow forever
Expected Behavior: Log rotates at 1MB: old log moved to reset-old.log on next write.

User Story: As a user I want to see online/offline status in the menu
Expected Behavior: Menu shows [ONLINE] green or [OFFLINE] red via Test-Network before each iteration.

User Story: As a user I want to inspect current TCP settings from the menu
Expected Behavior: Shows: CongestionProvider, Timestamps, InitialRTO, RSS, Autotuning, Dynamic Ports, Registry values, Optimization Targets.

User Story: As a user I want to re-run optimization without adapter reset
Expected Behavior: Calls Optimize-NetworkSpeed directly. Post-check + auto-rollback applies.

User Story: As a user I want to force full reset from the menu
Expected Behavior: Calls Invoke-NetworkReset. Includes VPN disable, IP renew, DNS flush, network check, optimize.

User Story: As a user I want to undo changes and return to menu (not exit)
Expected Behavior: Calls Invoke-Restore. Returns to menu loop (does not force quit).

User Story: As a user I want clean exit from the menu
Expected Behavior: Breaks menu loop, script ends.

User Story: As a user I want auto-rollback if optimization breaks my internet
Expected Behavior: After optimization: waits 5s, tests network. If down: calls Invoke-Restore automatically. Shows result.

User Story: As a user I want WiFi to auto-reconnect after network reset
Expected Behavior: Saves SSID to snapshot. After reset: reads snapshot, uses netsh wlan connect. Falls back to manual if no SSID.

User Story: As a user I want both IPv4 and IPv6 dynamic ports optimized
Expected Behavior: Both set to 49152-65535 (IANA recommended range).

User Story: As a user I want graceful degradation when components fail
Expected Behavior: Each step wrapped in try/catch. Failures logged as warnings, never crash the script. Steps continue.
Errors Found: BUG-3: Save-Snapshot had redundant nested 'if ($Adapter)' blocks
Fix Applied: Removed redundant inner 'if ($Adapter)' - now single block handles both DNS and SSID
